definitions.definition: $SANDBOX is the per-learner working directory at $HOME/.shelltutor (overridable via SHELLTUTOR_HOME). setup_sandbox() seeds fixtures (poem.txt, numbers.txt) and HISTFILE.

citations.evidence: SANDBOX="${SHELLTUTOR_HOME:-$HOME/.shelltutor}"
setup_sandbox() { mkdir -p ... }

definitions.definition: Welcome screen frames the sandbox as a complete safety guarantee for the learner.

citations.evidence: Everything happens in ~/.shelltutor; you cannot break anything

definitions.definition: Sandbox boundaries are part of the tutor's safety surface; changes require operator direction.

citations.evidence: tutor's safety surface (... sandbox boundaries)

definitions.definition: Lessons paired with safe, sandboxed practice.

citations.evidence: lessons paired with safe, sandboxed practice

terms.collision_note: Narrow ($SANDBOX directory, accurate) vs wide ('you cannot break anything', overstated — practice subshell holds learner's real filesystem authority). Confirms profile flag subshell-safety-claim-vs-shell-authority; full analysis Phase 6.

WHAT'S A PROMPT? The text the shell shows when ready

operator-name, distro-specific, or prompt-theme assumptions

terms.note: Within the project, all 'prompt' meanings are shell-related. No LLM-prompt collision; Phase 8 records prompts.count=0.

definitions.definition: Across Linux and macOS without environment assumptions; bash 4+ floor.

citations.evidence: POSIX-ish terminal (any modern Linux or macOS terminal works)
Stay portable across Linux and macOS

definitions.definition: Username/hostname/distro/shell-theme dependency = portability bug.

citations.evidence: Treat anything that requires a specific username, hostname, distro, or shell theme as a portability bug
Lessons must be runnable end-to-end on a clean Linux or macOS terminal

definitions.definition: Conditional rule: gate non-portable surfaces on runtime check; do not couple default path.

citations.evidence: If a lesson genuinely requires a non-portable surface, gate it on a runtime check and degrade gracefully

definitions.definition: Lesson uses Linux-only constructs without runtime gating: `free -h`, `/proc/cpuinfo`, `/proc/meminfo`, `ls /proc`.

citations.evidence: free -h ... /proc is Linux's self-report folder

definitions.definition: Lesson uses Fedora-only install hint without runtime gating: `sudo dnf install cowsay figlet lolcat`.

citations.evidence: sudo dnf install cowsay figlet lolcat

terms.collision_note: Governance claims Linux+macOS portability; lessons 7 and 8 use Linux-only and Fedora-only surfaces without the CONTRIBUTING.md:51-54 gate-and-fallback pattern. Confirms profile flag lesson-portability-gaps.

definitions.definition: One executable file with no installation step; readable in one sitting.

citations.evidence: single bash script with no installation step
Be readable as a single script
The tutor is one file on purpose

definitions.definition: Exactly one executable: shelltutor (459 lines).

citations.evidence: git ls-files shows shelltutor as the only executable; 9 tracked files total

definitions.definition: Bash function (welcome, lesson1-lesson9, finale); each renders a heredoc, then drops into the practice subshell.

citations.evidence: lesson() { clear...; cat; echo; practice; ...}
lessons=(welcome lesson1 lesson2 ... finale)

definitions.definition: Discrete teaching unit; ~15-command surface.

citations.evidence: Teach a learner the first ~15 shell commands

reference_doc_comparison.summary: Reference targets a Vimtutor-prereq goal; shelltutor's lesson surface is a subset by intentional scope (README non-goals 67-75). Not a finding; recorded for Phase 8 authoring-artifact context.
